title: lichen_l2_send and lichen_l2_input: buffer sizes assume no IPv6 extension headers
description: In lichen_l2.c lines 57-61, tx_ipv6_buf and rx_ipv6_buf are sized as LICHEN_L2_MTU + IPV6_BASE_HDR_LEN (240 bytes). The comment at lines 50-55 acknowledges this assumes no extension headers. If a packet with extension headers (e.g., Hop-by-Hop, OSCORE) arrives, it may exceed 240 bytes pre-compression and be rejected or truncated. This is documented in lichen_l2.h:38-39 but the failure mode is silent packet drop. When OSCORE is added (mentioned in spec), this will cause hard-to-debug packet loss. Consider: explicit size check with LOG_WRN naming extension headers as the likely cause, or bump buffers.
close_reason: Already documented in both lichen_l2.h (LICHEN_L2_MTU doc comment lines 33-37) and lichen_l2.c (buffer comment lines 50-55)
